channel.title: Frontier Flat Rate — Own Your AI
channel.description: AI subscription costs are spiraling. Flat-rate tools let you own your AI instead of renting it. Proof points, guides, and exit strategies.

item.title: Your Copilot Budget Is a 3-Month Illusion
item.description: GitHub's promotional credits expire September 2026. Business users lose 61% of credits. Enterprise loses 64%. No annual lock-in available. Your real costs start in 3 months.

item.title: The AI Rent Receipt: What You Actually Spend on AI Subscriptions in 2026
item.description: GitHub Copilot goes 10x-50x. Claude Pro splits your credits. An itemized breakdown of what renting your AI actually costs — and what owning it looks like instead.

item.title: You're Renting Your AI. Here's How You Can Tell.
item.description: Anthropic restricted third-party agent access and dropped team-tier pricing overnight. Here's the pattern nobody's naming — and the 4 signs you're renting your AI instead of owning it.

item.title: Day One: The Rent Already Arrived
item.description: June 1, 2026. GitHub Copilot's token billing went live today. Bills jumped 10x-50x. The safety net is gone. Two vendors, two weeks, the same word: sustainable.

item.title: Two Vendors, Two Weeks, Two Price Hikes — How to Own Your AI Before June 15
item.description: GitHub Copilot goes 9x on June 1. Anthropic splits your Claude credits on June 15. Here's the 3-step playbook to own your AI before the rent goes up again.

item.title: The Opt-In Trap
item.description: Anthropic's June 15 credit split requires developers to actively claim credits they already pay for. If you don't opt in, your programmatic AI access stops. Here's the full picture.

item.title: The Safety Net They Removed
item.description: GitHub Copilot didn't just raise prices on June 1. They removed the fallback model, killed annual plans, and started billing you on two meters at once. Here's what that means.

item.title: The $2 Student Who Proved AI Billing Is Broken
item.description: A CS student burned through 200 Copilot credits in one morning — 10 to 20 requests. Whether you pay $2 or $100,000, rent is rent. 12 days until Anthropic changes your AI bill on June 15.

item.title: You're Not Tired of AI. You're Tired of Renting It.
item.description: AI fatigue is real — 1,700+ people upvoted 'I'm Tired of Talking to AI' on Hacker News. But the fatigue isn't with AI itself. It's with renting your intelligence from platforms that can change the terms, raise the price, or cut you off tomorrow.

item.title: The Landlord Is Selling the Building — Anthropic's IPO Proves AI Rent Is Structural
item.description: Anthropic filed for a $965 billion IPO on the same day GitHub switched to token billing. Revenue went from $9B to $47B in six months. Your rent is the IPO fuel.

item.title: June 15 Is Coming — Why Your AI Agent Might Stop Working
item.description: On June 15, Anthropic splits Claude subscription credits. OpenClaw, Cursor, and other third-party agents get a separate pool. Here's what breaks.

item.title: Claude Opus 4.8 and the Effort Tax: Why Your AI Vendor Controls How Hard Your AI Thinks
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8 adds effort control — your vendor now decides how much thinking your AI does. Fast mode costs 3x less, proving the base price is inflated. Here's why this is the clearest Rent signal yet.

item.title: Same Model, Same Treadmill: Opus 4.8 Effort Control
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8 adds effort control — pick how hard the model thinks. It's the clearest admission yet that token billing is fundamentally broken.

item.title: The $1 Trillion Invoice: Who's Paying for the AI Build-Out?
item.description: Big Tech spent $1 trillion on AI infrastructure. Now the bill is arriving — at your company, in your inbox, on your credit card. Josh Bersin explains who pays.

item.title: The $200 Door That Won't Open: Copilot Max and the Subscription Trap
item.description: GitHub launched Copilot Max at $200/month — but new users still can't sign up. 43 days of closed doors. The complete 6-step subscription trap, documented in real time.

item.title: The 24x Hidden Price Range — Copilot Auto Mode Charges 24x More Without Telling You
item.description: Copilot Auto Mode picks the model — and the price. Same task can cost 24x more depending on which model is selected. You have no visibility. June 15: Anthropic does the same thing to your credits.

item.title: The Failure Tax: Why You Pay Twice for AI
item.description: AI doesn't just fail — you pay for the failure AND the fix. Token billing means vendors profit from errors. Here's why the Failure Tax is the hidden cost nobody talks about.

item.title: Copilot Alternatives 2026: Flat-Rate AI Tools After Token Billing
item.description: GitHub Copilot token billing starts June 1, 2026. Compare 7 flat-rate AI coding alternatives — no tokens, no metering, no bill shock. Full pricing breakdown.

item.title: Copilot Billing Changes June 2026: What Developers Need to Know
item.description: GitHub Copilot switches to token billing June 1, 2026. Here's what changes, how much more you'll pay, and the best alternatives with flat-rate pricing.

item.title: Flat-Rate AI vs Token Billing: What Developers Actually Pay in 2026
item.description: Token billing, per-seat licensing, usage tiers — AI pricing is fracturing into two models. Here's what each one actually costs, which vendors use which, and why flat-rate AI is the missing middle developers have been waiting for.

item.title: When Does Local AI Beat Cloud AI? The 2026 Break-Even
item.description: Outsourcing plus local AI is now more economical than frontier lab APIs for most developer workflows. Here's the math, the break-even points, and when owning your AI infrastructure beats renting it.

item.title: Copilot Token Billing Starts June 1: Here's What Your Bill Will Actually Look Like
item.description: GitHub Copilot adds per-operation token billing on June 1, 2026. Here's what your bill will actually look like — and why the pattern is bigger than one tool.

item.title: What Happens Monday June 2: Your First Copilot Token Bill
item.description: June 2 is the first business day under Copilot token billing. Here's what developers will see, what it means, and why this pattern matters for every AI tool you use.

item.title: Your AI Just Got 10GB Smaller
item.description: Google quietly cut Gmail free storage from 15GB to 5GB. No announcement. No email. Just a support page edit. Here's what it means for your AI agent.

item.title: Google I/O 2026 Is Over: The Definitive Own vs Rent Scorecard
item.description: Post-I/O 2026 synthesis: every announcement, every hidden cost, and why owning your AI agent is the only rational move for solopreneurs after Google's agent blitz.
